title: Swamimalai Murugan Temple
tagline: Swamimalai Murugan Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Swaminatha Swamy Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to god Murugan.
overview.description: Swaminatha Swamy Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to god Murugan. It is located on the banks of a tributary of river Kaveri in Swamimalai near Kumbakonam, Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India. The temple is one of the Six Abodes of Murugan (Arupadaiveedugal). The temple is located atop a 60 ft (18 m)-high hillock with the shrines dedicated to Shiva (Sundareswarar) and Meenakshi (Parvati) is located at the foothill.

topPlaces.name: Swamimalai
topPlaces.description: Swamimalai is a panchayat town that is a suburb near Kumbakonam in Thanjavur District in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It lies on the banks of river Kaveri and is the site of the fourth of the six abodes of the Hindu deity Murugan (Kartikeya).

topPlaces.name: Kabartheeswarar Temple
topPlaces.description: Kapardeeswarar temple (also called Swetha Vinayagar Temple, Vellai Vinayagar temple, Valanchuzinathar temple) is a Hindu temple situated in the village of Thiruvalanchuzhi (also spelt as Thiruvalanjuli) near Swamimalai in Kumbakonam taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India. Shiva is worshiped as Kapardeeswarar and is represented by the lingam and his consort Parvati is depicted as Brihannayagi.

topPlaces.name: Alavandipuram
topPlaces.description: Alavandipuram is a village in the Papanasam taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Thimmakudi
topPlaces.description: Thimmakudi is a village in the Kumbakonam taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Thiru Aadanoor Temple
topPlaces.description: The Thiru Aadanoor or the Andalakkum Aiyan Perumal Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu located in Aadanoor, Thanjavur district in Tamil Nadu, India. Constructed in Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is counted as one among the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu.

topPlaces.name: Olaipadi
topPlaces.description: Olaipadi is a village in the Papanasam taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India. The village is situated on the northern side four km from Swamimalai near Kumbakonam and one km northeast from Adhanur, one of the 108 Divyadesams, has a temple for Lord Vishnu.

topPlaces.name: Valaiyapettai
topPlaces.description: Valaiyapettai is a village in the Kumbakonam taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Thirupullabhoothangudi Temple
topPlaces.description: Thirupullabhoothangudi Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu located in Pullabhoothangudi near Kumbakonam in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Constructed in South Indian Style of Architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is counted as one among the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu.

items.note: Swamimalai is a panchayat town that is a suburb near Kumbakonam in Thanjavur District in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It lies on the banks of river Kaveri and is the site of the fourth of the six abodes of the Hindu deity Murugan (Kartikeya).

Kapardeeswarar temple (also called Swetha Vinayagar Temple, Vellai Vinayagar temple, Valanchuzinathar temple) is a Hindu temple situated in the village of Thiruvalanchuzhi (also spelt as Thiruvalanjuli) near Swamimalai in Kumbakonam taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India. Shiva is worshiped as Kapardeeswarar and is represented by the lingam and his consort Parvati is depicted as Brihannayagi.
Alavandipuram is a village in the Papanasam taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India.
Thimmakudi is a village in the Kumbakonam taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India.

The Thiru Aadanoor or the Andalakkum Aiyan Perumal Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu located in Aadanoor, Thanjavur district in Tamil Nadu, India. Constructed in Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is counted as one among the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu.

Olaipadi is a village in the Papanasam taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India. The village is situated on the northern side four km from Swamimalai near Kumbakonam and one km northeast from Adhanur, one of the 108 Divyadesams, has a temple for Lord Vishnu.
Valaiyapettai is a village in the Kumbakonam taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India.

Thirupullabhoothangudi Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu located in Pullabhoothangudi near Kumbakonam in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Constructed in South Indian Style of Architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is counted as one among the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Swamimalai Murugan Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Swamimalai Murugan Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Swamimalai Murugan Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (71 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (72 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Swamimalai Murugan Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Swamimalai Murugan Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Swamimalai Murugan Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Swamimalai Murugan Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Swamimalai Murugan Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Swamimalai, Kabartheeswarar Temple, Alavandipuram, Thimmakudi, Thiru Aadanoor Temple.
